title: Optimized Macherey-Nagel NucleoSpin Tissue Protocol for Environmental DNA Extraction
author: Michaela Harris

steps.title: Install all the material on the...
steps.instruction: Install all the material on the benchtop including 2 mL microtubes with Lyse&Spin baskets for extraction, pre-identified 2 mL Eppendorf Safe-Lock for back up, tweezers, scissors, weigh boats, waste beaker, microtube opener, and gloves.

steps.title: Before use, clean bench with water, 0
steps.instruction: Before use, clean bench with water, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and water or with water, Alconox, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and ethanol.

steps.title: Above a clean weigh boat, take...
steps.instruction: Above a clean weigh boat, take a filter with clean tweezers, unfold it, and cut it in two equal halfs with clean scissors. Alternatively, use sterilized and disposable toothpicks and scalpels to transfer and cut filters in a weigh boat. Because filters were preserved with ethanol 95-100%, leave one half to air dry on the weigh boat. Note: Try to divide the filtrate equally on each half.

steps.title: Once air dried, roll the filter...
steps.instruction: Once air dried, roll the filter half (filtrate inside), and put it in the extraction microtube (2 mL microtube with a Lyse&Spin basket) using clean tweezers (or toothpicks and scalpel). If DNA extraction will occur on a subsequent day, put the filter half into a 2 mL microtube, and store it at -20 °C . If DNA extraction will occur on the same day, keep it at room temperature on the bench.

steps.title: Roll the second half of the...
steps.instruction: Roll the second half of the filter with tweezers, put it into the Eppendorf Safe-Lock 2 mL previously identified, and store it as back up at -80 °C . Alternatively, store the second half filter in its original tubes containing ethanol 95-100% at -20 °C .

steps.title: If not using disposable equipment, rinse...
steps.instruction: If not using disposable equipment, rinse tweezers, scissors, and weigh boats with water, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and water for final rinse during at least 00:00:30 , and ethanol (i.e., to dry equipment and avoid rust). Change gloves. Repeat steps 3 to 6 for each filter.

steps.title: DO NOT TOUCH microtube edges with gloves while opening
steps.instruction: DO NOT TOUCH microtube edges with gloves while opening. Always use a microtube opener. In case of doubt for contamination, change gloves before touching another sample. DO NOT OPEN more than one microtube at a time to limit contamination. Alternatively, if using a multidispense pipette, leave space between opened tubes. ALWAYS do a quick spin before microtube opening to limit aerosols. In case of doubt for contamination, rinse the microtube opener with water, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and water. Optimally, change the pipette tip between microtubes when adding a solution, even if the same solution is added. Alternatively, use a multidispense pipette with sterile tips, not changing pipette tips while adding the same solution.

steps.title: Change gloves and work under extractor...
steps.instruction: Change gloves and work under extractor hood or arm for all subsequent steps because of proteinase K (i.e., may cause breathing difficulties if inhaled).

steps.title: Prepare a mastermix of 360 µL...
steps.instruction: Prepare a mastermix of 360 µL of T1 solution and 50 µL of proteinase K to dispense to each microtube. NOTE: Work as quickly as possible once proteinase K is mixed to T1 to avoid self digestion.

steps.title: Mix microtubes by inversion for few seconds
steps.instruction: Mix microtubes by inversion for few seconds. Make sure that the filter stays immerged in the solution.

steps.title: Place microtubes in the thermomixer
steps.instruction: Place microtubes in the thermomixer. Incubate Overnight at 56 °C with shaking at 900 rpm to digest the filtrate. NOTE: GF filters should not digest. NOTE: If not overnight, incubation should last at least 03:00:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge microtubes at 18,000 g during 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge microtubes at 18,000 g during 00:01:00 . NOTE: If lysis solution is still present in the column after centrifugation, redo step 17 until all the solution went through the column. Make note in an excel sheet.

steps.title: To avoid leaking issues, transfer the...
steps.instruction: To avoid leaking issues, transfer the eluate to a new labeled 2 mL microtube.

steps.title: Add 400 µL of B3 buffer to each microtube
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of B3 buffer to each microtube. Change pipette tip between each microtube or use a multidispense pipette with sterile tips, not changing pipette tips. Mix microtubes by inversion for few seconds or vortex microtubes for few seconds.

steps.title: Incubate in the thermomixer for 00:10:00 at 70 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate in the thermomixer for 00:10:00 at 70 °C .

steps.title: Quick spin
steps.instruction: Quick spin.

steps.title: Add 420 µL of ethanol 95-100% to each microtube
steps.instruction: Add 420 µL of ethanol 95-100% to each microtube. Change pipette tip between each microtube or use a multidispense pipette with sterile tips, not changing pipette tips.

steps.title: Mix microtubes by inversion for few...
steps.instruction: Mix microtubes by inversion for few seconds or vortex microtubes for few seconds.

steps.title: Quick spin
steps.instruction: Quick spin.

steps.title: Pipet up to 650 µL of...
steps.instruction: Pipet up to 650 µL of the mix into a MN column placed in a 2 mL collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 11,000 g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 11,000 g. Discard flow-through. NOTE: When flow-through is discarded in the trash, be careful not to contaminate gloves and bench top.

steps.title: Repeat steps 25 to 26 until...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 25 to 26 until all the solution has passed through the column. Change the collection tube. NOTE: DNA is on the column.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of BW buffer
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of BW buffer. Change pipette tip between each microtube or use a multidispense pipette with sterile tips, not changing pipette tips.

steps.title: Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 11,000 g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 11,000 g. Discard flow-through and collection tube.

steps.title: Add 600 µL of B5 buffer
steps.instruction: Add 600 µL of B5 buffer. Change pipette tip between each microtube or use a multidispense pipette with sterile tips, not changing pipette tips.

steps.title: Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 11,000 g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 11,000 g. Discard flow-through.

steps.title: Centrifuge again at 11,000 g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge again at 11,000 g for 00:01:00 to dry the column.

steps.title: Place the column into a 2...
steps.instruction: Place the column into a 2 mL labeled Eppendorf Safe-Lock microtube.

steps.title: Add 50 µL of BE buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of BE buffer (previously heated at 70 °C ) on the center of the membrane.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Repeat steps 34 to 36 for...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 34 to 36 for a second round of elution.

steps.title: Discard the column and store the...
steps.instruction: Discard the column and store the 2 mL labeled Eppendorf Safe-Lock microtube with the eluate at 4 °C for short-term storage, at -20 °C for medium-term storage, and at -80 °C for long-term storage. NOTE: The storage protocol of DNA extracts maximizes chances of eDNA detections since freeze-thaw cycles limit detection of rare molecules (i.e., most likely due to deterioration of DNA's integrity). We favour the storage of DNA extracts within the ultraclean laboratory at -20 °C to limit contamination. We favour the long term storage of DNA extracts at -80 °C to maximize the preservation of DNA integrity, when no detections are planned.

steps.title: Clean bench before use with water (i
steps.instruction: Clean bench before use with water (i.e., Milli-Q water, hereafter "water" for cleaning procedures, to remove Macherey-Nagel solutions with guanidine salts prior bleaching; see warnings for details), 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution (i.e., to degrade DNA), and water (i.e. to rinse traces of sodium hypochlorite). Alternatively, clean bench before use with Alconox, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution and ethanol 70% (hereafter referred to ethanol for cleaning procedures).

steps.title: Clean bench with water, 0
steps.instruction: Clean bench with water, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and water or with water, Alconox, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and ethanol.

steps.title: Clean pipettes and centrifuges by wiping...
steps.instruction: Clean pipettes and centrifuges by wiping them down with water, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, water, and ethanol.

steps.title: Install all the material on the...
steps.instruction: Install all the material on the benchtop including microtubes, reagents, collection tubes from the Macherey-Nagel NucleoSpin Tissue kit, and racks.

steps.title: Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 11,000 g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 11,000 g.

steps.title: Throw liquid bench top trash into...
steps.instruction: Throw liquid bench top trash into the liquid trash of the laboratory.

steps.title: Rinse the liquid bench top trash...
steps.instruction: Rinse the liquid bench top trash with water, discard this water in the liquid trash of the laboratory, then clean it with water, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and water.

steps.title: Clean bench with water, 0
steps.instruction: Clean bench with water, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and water or with water, Alconox, 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution, and ethanol.

steps.title: Prepare an extraction negative control for each extraction day
steps.instruction: Prepare an extraction negative control for each extraction day. Humidify the filter with 250 µL of Milli-Q water, add 250 µL of ethanol 95-100%, and proceed with steps 3 and 4. Alternatively, the extraction negative control could be prepared at step 14 by adding T1 solution only. Note 1: Use a filter of material and porosity identical to those from the project.
